steps.title: Find the roots and the vertex x-coordinate
steps.content:
The function $f(x) = (2x - 20)(2x + 8)$ is in factored form. The roots occur where each factor equals zero:
$$2x - 20 = 0 \Rightarrow x = 10$$$$2x + 8 = 0 \Rightarrow x = -4$$
The vertex of a parabola lies midway between its roots. Since the leading coefficient is positive ($(2x) \cdot (2x) = 4x^2 > 0$), the parabola opens upward and the vertex represents a minimum:
$$x = \frac{10 + (-4)}{2} = 3$$

steps.title: Evaluate the function at the vertex
steps.content:
Substitute $x = 3$ into the original expression:
$$f(3) = (2(3) - 20)(2(3) + 8)$$$$= (6 - 20)(6 + 8)$$$$= (-14)(14)$$$$= -196$$
The minimum value of the function is $-196$.
